code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a monument into a single fluent sentence.
    Triples sharing the same subject are merged into a coordinated clause.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a natural‑language phrase (use {obj} placeholder)
    pred_map = {
        "category": "is a {obj}",
        "country": "is located in {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "municipality": "lies in the municipality of {obj}",
        "state": "lies in the state of {obj}",
        "district": "belongs to the {obj} district",
        "established": "was established on {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "owningOrganisation": "is owned by {obj}",
        "dedicatedTo": "is dedicated to {obj}",
        "designer": "was designed by {obj}",
        "material": "is made of {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has an ethnic group of {obj}",
        "language": "uses the {obj} language",
        "religion": "has the religion {obj}",
        "nearestCity": "is near {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the {obj}",
        "largestCity": "has its largest city {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has the title of {obj}",
        "capital": "has the capital {obj}",
        "legislature": "has the legislature {obj}",
        "hasToItsNorth": "has to its north {obj}",
        "hasToItsSouthwest": "has to its southwest {obj}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west {obj}",
        "hasToItsSoutheast": "has to its southeast {obj}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject
    subject_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        phrase_template = pred_map.get(t.predicate, "has {obj}")
        phrase = phrase_template.format(obj=t.object)
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build coordinated clauses for each subject
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subject, phrases in subject_map.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            # join with commas and an "and" before the last phrase
            *initial, last = phrases
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(initial) + f", and {last}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join subject clauses into one sentence
    sentence = " Also, ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Ensure the first character is capitalized
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
